rationale: The cited paper focuses on how repeated elicitation questions can induce adaptation and reduce external validity, which is methodologically plausible for the citing paper’s mix of repeated-trial lab tasks and one-shot online choice designs. However, the provided notes do not show a direct conceptual link to the citing paper’s core default-mechanism question (golden halo/attention vs starting bias), and the raw evidence is only a single DOI match with no in-text context. Given this sparse and mixed evidence, it is not safe to drop but not strong enough to clearly keep.
